Round 1.title: Programming MCQ Quiz
Round 1.para: In this round, teams will take part in a multiple-choice quiz designed to assess their fundamental programming knowledge and logical thinking skills. Questions will cover a wide range of programming concepts. For every correct answer, teams will earn coins, which will play a crucial role in the next round.
Round 1.para1: Only teams with the highest coin counts will gain a strategic advantage moving forward.

Round 2.title: Code Auction
Round 2.para: Teams will use the coins earned in Round 1 to bid in an auction for essential coding components. The exact coding problems will not be revealed before the auction, though general hints may be provided.
Round 2.para1: All items are limited, non-exchangeable, and must be used wisely.

Round 3.title: Coding Challenge
Round 3.para: Teams must solve the given programming problems using only the components they purchased during the auction.
list: Basic syntax and input/output operations are free to use
Solutions will be evaluated based on correctness and efficiency
Round 3.para1: Teams must manage their resources carefully to produce the best possible solution within the given time.

event_rules: Team Size : 2 members only
Use of any AI tools is not allowed.
The judge's decision is final.
event_requirements: Valid identification and proof of registration are required.
Bring pen.

Round 1.title: Player Screening (Preliminary Logic Escape)
Round 1.para: Teams of two solve 8–10 sequential mini-puzzles focused on logic, pattern recognition, cipher decoding, and computer science aptitude within 30 minutes.
Round 1.para1: The first 10 teams to solve all puzzles qualify for Round 2.

Round 2.title: Crewmate Technical
Round 2.para: An advanced logical round with interconnected puzzles and minimal algorithm tracing.
Round 2.para1: Top 5 teams advance based on accuracy, problem-solving flow, and time taken.

Round 3.title: Ultimate Escape Arena
Round 3.para: An immersive physical and logical escape challenge conducted inside a decorated classroom where teams search for hidden clues and solve interconnected tasks.
Round 3.para1: The fastest and most accurate team to successfully escape wins.

event_rules: Each team must consist of 2 members.
AI tools are not allowed.
All tasks must be completed within the given time limit.
Judges’ decision will be final and binding.
event_requirements: Valid identification and proof of registration are required.
Pen and paper required.

Round 1.para: This paper presentation event offers students, professionals, and researchers a platform to present innovative ideas and research across diverse technology domains.
Round 1.para1: Domains include Artificial Intelligence, Cybersecurity, Data Science, Machine Learning, Blockchain, Internet of Things (IoT), and Cloud Computing.

event_rules: Each team must consist of 2 to 3 members.
Participants must present within the domain assigned by the panel.
Any unethical behaviour will result in immediate disqualification.
Judges’ decisions are final and binding.
event_requirements: Valid ID proof and registration confirmation are mandatory.
Participants must bring their own laptops.

Round 1.title: Task Phase & Strategy
Round 1.para: Among Bytes is a technical game inspired by Among Us, combining coding challenges, logical reasoning, and psychological strategy.
list: Each team consists of 4 members (3 Crewmates, 1 Impostor)
Impostor is assigned randomly and confidentially
Crewmates must complete assigned technical tasks
Impostor must mislead without obvious sabotage
Round 1.para1: Success depends on collaboration, observation, and strategic decision-making.

event_rules: Only team discussion is allowed.
Internet usage is not allowed unless stated.
All tasks must be completed within time.
Ethical gameplay is mandatory.
event_requirements: Valid identification and proof of registration are required.
Laptop is necessary.

Round 1.title: Generative AI + RAG Workshop
Round 1.para: A hands-on workshop exploring Generative AI and Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG). Participants will build a student records and academics AI application.
list: Building an AI-powered academic query system
Round 1.para1: Participants gain practical skills for real-world AI applications.

event_rules: Participants must actively engage in hands-on activities.
Basic Python knowledge required.
event_requirements: Valid identification and proof of registration required.
Laptop necessary.
Required software must be pre-installed.

Round 1.title: Cyber Bingo
Round 1.para: A Bingo-based cybersecurity quiz testing theoretical understanding.
list: Correct Bingo qualifies for next round

Round 2.title: Capture The Flag (CTF)
Round 2.para: Teams solve 4–5 cybersecurity challenges involving vulnerabilities and decoding.
Round 2.para1: Evaluation based on accuracy, difficulty, and completion time.

event_rules: Team size: 2-3 members.
AI tools are not allowed.
All tasks must be completed within time.
Judges’ decision is final.
event_requirements: Valid identification and proof of registration required.

Round 1.title: Binary Breach Gate
Round 1.para: Teams decode binary messages and answer technical questions to obtain Key 1.

Round 2.title: Logic and Math Gate
Round 2.para: Teams solve number patterns and reasoning challenges to obtain Key 2.

Round 3.title: Cipher Lock Gate
Round 3.para: Teams decode encrypted Caesar or substitution ciphers to obtain Key 3.

event_rules: Each team must consist of exactly 2 members.
Mobile phones are strictly prohibited.
Judges’ decision is final.
event_requirements: Laptop is necessary.
Valid identification and proof of registration required.
Bring pen.

Round 1.title: App Idea Pitch
Round 1.para: In this round, participants will present a quick and creative verbal pitch based on randomly assigned app categories. This round is designed to evaluate creativity, logical thinking, and communication skills without focusing on written tests or drawing abilities.
list: Conceptualize an app by combining given categories
Present idea within a short time frame
Round 1.para1: Only the top-performing teams will qualify for the next round.

Round 2.title: UI Design Challenge
Round 2.para: Participants will design the user interface for the same app idea pitched in Round 1 using approved design tools. Teams must translate their concept into a visually appealing and user-friendly UI.
list: Align UI with original app idea
Round 2.para1: Designs will be evaluated on visual aesthetics, creativity, and overall user experience within the given duration.

event_rules: Team size must be 1–2 participants only.
Participants must use only approved design tools (Figma / Adobe XD / Sketch / Canva).
Original work only — pre-made templates, previous projects, or copied designs are strictly prohibited.
Internet usage allowed only for basic assets unless stated otherwise.
Shortlisted teams must continue with the same app idea in Round 2.
Judges’ decision will be final and binding.
event_requirements: Laptop is necessary with installed required software.
Valid identification and proof of registration are required.
Pen and paper.

Round 1.title: Decode the Logic. Navigate the System. Gain Access.
Round 1.para: Root Access is a single-round, logic-driven technical challenge that tests participants’ programming skills, logical reasoning, and system-level understanding.
list: Correct terminal commands and directory navigation
Round 1.para1: Teams must analyze problem statements, validate using dummy input, generate an access key, and navigate hidden directories using the terminal. The team retrieving the maximum valid keywords within the time limit wins.

event_rules: Each team must consist of exactly 2 members.
Participants may use any programming language available on the system.
Participants must use the terminal to write, compile, and execute their code.
Participants must validate their code using dummy input before receiving actual input.
Internet usage is strictly prohibited.
Mobile phones, internet, or external storage devices are not allowed.
Participants may only access directories using valid generated keys.
Participants must not modify, delete, or tamper with system folders or files.
Teams must work independently.
event_requirements: Valid identification and proof of registration are required.
Bring notebook and pen.
